title: apps/api/tsconfig.json is NOT updated to reference the new spec config.
body_excerpt:
apps/api/tsconfig.json is NOT updated to reference the new spec config.

apps/mobile/tsconfig.json includes the spec path in its references array; apps/api/tsconfig.json does not. Without this, tsc --build (composite project graph) never visits the spec config.
evidence: apps/api/tsconfig.json line 10 now contains { "path": "./tsconfig.spec.json" } alongside tsconfig.app.json. Fixed in commit 468ae424f ('cfg(api): close tsconfig.spec.json strict settings gap [PR-15f]') which explicitly added the reference and removed noUnusedLocals/noUncheckedIndexedAccess relaxations.

title: Every other package/app in this monorepo that ships a `tsconfig.spec.json` lists it in its root `tsconfig.json` references array:
body_excerpt:
Every other package/app in this monorepo that ships a `tsconfig.spec.json` lists it in its root `tsconfig.json` references array:

| File | References spec? |
|---|---|
| `packages/database/tsconfig.json` | YES `./tsconfig.spec.json` |
evidence: apps/api/tsconfig.json now has tsconfig.spec.json in its references (line 10). The follow-up commit 468ae424f also removed the noUnusedLocals:false and noUncheckedIndexedAccess:false relaxations, closing both the missing-reference and the silent-strictness-gap issues simultaneously.

title: apps/api/tsconfig.json is not updated
body_excerpt:
_apps/api/tsconfig.json is not updated_

The new tsconfig.spec.json is never added to the root apps/api/tsconfig.json references array. In TypeScript composite/project-references mode, tsc --build (and nx run api:typecheck) walks the graph from the root tsconfig
evidence: apps/api/tsconfig.json at line 10 now includes { "path": "./tsconfig.spec.json" }. Commit 468ae424f added the reference and fixed 180+ downstream TS errors to make the spec config properly visible to tsc --build.

title: Self-reported BLOCK finding not disclosed
body_excerpt:
_Self-reported BLOCK finding not disclosed_

Per CLAUDE.md: "NEVER dismiss review failures as OK to merge." The PR body surfaces the verdict (BLOCK / 1C / 1M / 1L) but does not list the actual findings.
evidence: The PR was merged only after follow-up commits (468ae424f) addressed the critical tsconfig.spec.json gap including 180+ type errors. The process of resolving the BLOCK finding and subsequent round-2 fixes demonstrates the issues were enumerated and resolved rather than dismissed.

title: noUnusedLocals: false diverges from the mobile precedent without justification.
body_excerpt:
noUnusedLocals: false diverges from the mobile precedent without justification.

apps/mobile/tsconfig.spec.json keeps noUnusedLocals: true. The PR attributes the 279-error deferral to noUncheckedIndexedAccess, but this also silently disables unused-local checking
evidence: apps/api/tsconfig.spec.json no longer has noUnusedLocals: false or noUncheckedIndexedAccess: false — both relaxations were removed by commit 468ae424f which fixed the underlying errors instead. The spec config now inherits noUnusedLocals: true from tsconfig.base.json, matching mobile's behavior.

title: Covered in the inline comment on `apps/api/tsconfig.spec.json` lines 3–11.
body_excerpt:
Covered in the inline comment on `apps/api/tsconfig.spec.json` lines 3–11.

---
evidence: The tsconfig.spec.json deferred-relaxation comments were removed in commit 468ae424f, which resolved the underlying errors. The inline comment block no longer exists in the current file, which only has outDir and types fields.

title: The updated rule says "The removed personaFromBirthYear()" but 13 files in apps/mobile/src still import it
body_excerpt: The updated rule says "The removed personaFromBirthYear()" but 13 files in apps/mobile/src still import it (tracked as KNOWN_SITES in persona-fossil-guard.test.ts). The fossil guard already enforces the migration correctly; the CLAUDE.md wording just needs to match reality
evidence: CLAUDE.md line 45 was updated to 'deprecated/under migration' by commit c14cd40b4, then updated again to 'removed' by commit 47f830ed0 once PR-11/P7 landed and fully removed personaFromBirthYear(). The current wording 'The removed personaFromBirthYear()' is now accurate — the function is no longer present in the codebase.

title: The established observer pattern in `ask-classification-observe.ts` calls `captureException` (Sentry) in the schema drift path
body_excerpt: The established observer pattern in `ask-classification-observe.ts` calls `captureException` (Sentry) in the schema drift path in addition to the logger call, so schema drift is traceable and alertable in Sentry, not just in log queries. The three new observers (`askGateDecisionObserve`, `askGateTimeoutObserve`, `emailBouncedObserve`) only call `logger.error`.
evidence: ask-gate-observe.ts lines 53-57 and 90-94 call logger.error only — no captureException. email-bounced-observe.ts lines 42-45 similarly calls logger.error only. ask-classification-observe.ts lines 41-46 and 77-82 still call captureException. The three new observers remain inconsistent with the established pattern.
suggested_notion_title: Observer schema-drift paths missing captureException (Sentry alert gap)
suggested_notion_body: askGateDecisionObserve, askGateTimeoutObserve, and emailBouncedObserve call logger.error on schema_drift but not captureException, unlike the sibling ask-classification-observe.ts. On-call will not receive a Sentry alert if these observers' payloads drift. Fix: add captureException() in all three schema_drift paths, matching ask-classification-observe.ts lines 41-46.

title: The log message in `emailBouncedObserve` is always `'email.bounced.received'` even when `data.type` is `'email.complained'`
body_excerpt: The log message in `emailBouncedObserve` is always `'email.bounced.received'` even when `data.type` is `'email.complained'`. The event type is present in the `context.type` field, but anyone querying logs for `message = 'email.complained.received'` will get zero results.
evidence: email-bounced-observe.ts line 50 still hardcodes logger.warn('email.bounced.received', ...) regardless of data.type. The type is logged in the context field but the message name is never dynamic. Querying logs for 'email.complained.received' returns zero results.
suggested_notion_title: emailBouncedObserve log message always 'email.bounced.received' regardless of event type
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/inngest/functions/email-bounced-observe.ts line 50 hardcodes the log message as 'email.bounced.received' even when data.type is 'email.complained'. Log queries for 'email.complained.received' return zero results. Fix: use `${data.type ?? 'email.bounced'}.received` as the message string so complaint and bounce events are distinguishable in log queries.

title: Both new observer files log schema drift via logger.error but do not call captureException.
body_excerpt: Both new observer files log schema drift via logger.error but do not call captureException. The nearest sibling ask-classification-observe.ts calls captureException in its schema_drift path so the failure surfaces in Sentry alerting. Without it, schema drift in the new observers is invisible to on-call monitoring.
evidence: This is the same antipattern as comment-4434360279 (above). ask-gate-observe.ts and email-bounced-observe.ts both lack captureException in their schema_drift paths. The pattern violation is still present in the current codebase.

title: askGateDecisionPayloadSchema, askGateTimeoutPayloadSchema, and emailBouncedPayloadSchema are defined inline in the observer files.
body_excerpt: askGateDecisionPayloadSchema, askGateTimeoutPayloadSchema, and emailBouncedPayloadSchema are defined inline in the observer files. The established pattern (set by ask-classification-observe.ts) is to define these in packages/schemas/src/inngest-events.ts and import them.
evidence: Grep of packages/schemas/src/inngest-events.ts finds no askGateDecisionPayloadSchema, askGateTimeoutPayloadSchema, or emailBouncedPayloadSchema exports. All three schemas remain inline in ask-gate-observe.ts (lines 29-42) and email-bounced-observe.ts (lines 26-31), not in the shared schemas package.
suggested_notion_title: Observer payload schemas not promoted to @eduagent/schemas (single source of truth gap)
suggested_notion_body: askGateDecisionPayloadSchema, askGateTimeoutPayloadSchema, and emailBouncedPayloadSchema are defined inline in their observer files rather than in packages/schemas/src/inngest-events.ts. The event emitter (sessions.ts, resend-webhook.ts) and consumer (observers) share no TypeScript contract. Schema drift between sender and receiver will not be caught by tsc. Fix: export schemas from @eduagent/schemas and import them in both the emitter and observer.

title: createSubject(profileId, name) always builds headers from SUBJECT_AUTH_USER_ID / SUBJECT_AUTH_EMAIL, even when passed otherProfileId.
body_excerpt: createSubject(profileId, name) always builds headers from SUBJECT_AUTH_USER_ID / SUBJECT_AUTH_EMAIL, even when passed otherProfileId. The new cross-profile test only ever calls it with the first user's ownerProfileId so the test is correct today, but the helper is a footgun
evidence: tests/integration/subject-management.integration.test.ts line 93-96 now shows createSubject accepts a third `user` parameter with default `{ userId: SUBJECT_AUTH_USER_ID, email: SUBJECT_AUTH_EMAIL }`. The helper's buildAuthHeaders call at line 105-108 uses the passed user object, not the hardcoded constant. The footgun was fixed in follow-up commit c29088286.

title: `createHookWrapper` provides a `ProfileContext.Provider` whose default profile has `id: 'test-profile-id'`, but it does **not** call `setActiveProfileId()`
body_excerpt: `createHookWrapper` provides a `ProfileContext.Provider` whose default profile has `id: 'test-profile-id'`, but it does **not** call `setActiveProfileId()` to sync the api-client module-level `_activeProfileId`. This test works because `beforeEach` manually calls `setActiveProfileId('test-profile-id')`
evidence: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-progress.test.ts: createHookWrapper is called at line 61 but setActiveProfileId is only called separately in beforeEach at line 36. The coupling between ProfileContext and the api-client module-level state is not addressed inside createHookWrapper itself — callers must still remember to call setActiveProfileId separately, preserving the footgun.
suggested_notion_title: createHookWrapper does not sync setActiveProfileId — test correctness footgun
suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-progress.test.ts uses createHookWrapper for ProfileContext but relies on beforeEach calling setActiveProfileId separately to sync the api-client module-level _activeProfileId. If a test forgets beforeEach or calls createHookWrapper with a different profileId, requests will silently use the wrong profile. Fix: have createHookWrapper call setActiveProfileId internally when an activeProfile is provided.

title: (1) TopicPickerSheet.test.tsx lines 60-74: implicit light-mode dependency without ThemeContext.Provider
body_excerpt:
(1) TopicPickerSheet.test.tsx lines 60-74: implicit light-mode dependency without ThemeContext.Provider

The two style-asserting tests check tokens.light.colors.primarySoft and tokens.light.colors.surface but never wrap in ThemeContext.Provider. ThemeContext defaults to colorScheme: light so tests p
evidence: apps/mobile/src/components/library/TopicPickerSheet.test.tsx lines 60-74 still assert tokens.light.colors.primarySoft and tokens.light.colors.surface without a ThemeContext.Provider wrapper. No ThemeContext or ThemeProvider import exists in the file. If the default colorScheme ever changes to dark, these tests will silently pass on wrong values.
suggested_notion_title: TopicPickerSheet tests have implicit light-mode dependency (no ThemeContext.Provider)
suggested_notion_body: TopicPickerSheet.test.tsx lines 60-73 assert tokens.light.colors.primarySoft/surface without wrapping in ThemeContext.Provider. Tests rely on the implicit default colorScheme: 'light'. Fix: wrap token-asserting tests in a ThemeContext.Provider with explicit colorScheme: 'light', or use the theme-aware token path instead of the hardcoded .light. bucket.

title: The job-level comment at line 55 still reads: PR / push-to-main: lightweight lint+typecheck
body_excerpt:
The job-level comment at line 55 still reads:
> PR / push-to-main: lightweight lint+typecheck (CI ran full suite on PR).

But after this PR, the `api-quality-gate` job on PRs now also spins up a postgres service container and runs `drizzle-kit migrate`
evidence: .github/workflows/deploy.yml line 52 still reads: '# Push-to-main: lightweight lint+typecheck (CI ran full suite on PR).' but the api-quality-gate job below it spins up postgres (line 59) and runs drizzle-kit migrate (line 97). The comment no longer accurately describes the job's scope.
suggested_notion_title: deploy.yml api-quality-gate job comment is stale (says 'lightweight' but runs postgres+migrations)
suggested_notion_body: .github/workflows/deploy.yml line 52 comment says 'Push-to-main: lightweight lint+typecheck' but the job runs a postgres service (line 59) and drizzle-kit migrate (line 97). The stale comment may mislead contributors adding steps to this job. Fix: update the comment to reflect the actual job scope.

title: [Medium] Postgres service starts on every PR even though unit/integration tests are skipped
body_excerpt:
[Medium] Postgres service starts on every PR even though unit/integration tests are skipped -- see deploy.yml line 59 inline comment.

[Low] createNote.mutate mock uses unknown rest args cast -- see SessionFooter.test.tsx line 143 inline comment.
evidence: deploy.yml job 'api-quality-gate' has trigger condition: 'if: github.event_name == ''push'' || (github.event_name == ''workflow_dispatch'' ...)' — it does NOT run on pull_request events. PRs use the separate api-quality-gate.yml workflow which has no postgres service. The postgres service in deploy.yml only starts on push-to-main and workflow_dispatch.

title: `let queryClient: QueryClient` is typed non-nullable, but `afterEach` uses `queryClient?.clear()`
body_excerpt: `let queryClient: QueryClient` is typed non-nullable, but `afterEach` uses `queryClient?.clear()` -- optional chaining that only makes sense if the variable can be undefined. The variable is assigned only inside `createWrapper()`, so if a test throws before `renderHook`, the optional chain silently
evidence: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-progress.test.ts line 29: 'let queryClient: QueryClient' is typed non-nullable. Line 40: 'queryClient?.clear()' uses optional chaining. The variable is only assigned inside createWrapper() at line 63. If a test throws before createWrapper() is called, the optional chain silently skips cleanup, leaving the queryClient in a potentially dirty state for subsequent tests.
suggested_notion_title: use-progress.test.ts: queryClient typed non-nullable but uses optional chaining in afterEach
suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-progress.test.ts line 29 declares 'let queryClient: QueryClient' (non-nullable) but afterEach at line 40 uses 'queryClient?.clear()' (optional chain). If a test throws before createWrapper() assigns queryClient, cleanup is silently skipped. Fix: declare as 'let queryClient: QueryClient | undefined' to align the type with the optional chain, or initialize to a fallback QueryClient at declaration.

title: Dead `icon` prop in `SubjectTile` (incomplete refactor)
body_excerpt:
**Dead `icon` prop in `SubjectTile` (incomplete refactor)**

`icon` was removed from the component's destructuring but remains required (non-optional) in `SubjectTileProps`. `Ionicons` is now imported solely to type a prop that the component never reads.
evidence: SubjectTile.tsx line 2 still imports Ionicons from @expo/vector-icons. Line 15 still declares 'icon: React.ComponentProps<typeof Ionicons>['name']' as required in SubjectTileProps. The destructuring does not include icon (line 20-31). LearnerScreen.tsx lines 73-95 still pass icon fields. DEFAULT_SUBJECT_ICON still referenced at line 295. The refactor is incomplete.
suggested_notion_title: SubjectTile incomplete refactor: dead required icon prop, orphaned Ionicons import, and LearnerScreen callers still pass icon
suggested_notion_body: apps/mobile/src/components/home/SubjectTile.tsx still declares icon as a required prop (line 15) and imports Ionicons (line 2) even though icon is not destructured or used in the JSX. LearnerScreen.tsx still defines DEFAULT_SUBJECT_ICON and passes icon: DEFAULT_SUBJECT_ICON for every card. Fix: remove icon from SubjectTileProps, remove the Ionicons import from SubjectTile.tsx, remove DEFAULT_SUBJECT_ICON and the icon field from the subjectCards useMemo in LearnerScreen.tsx.

title: library.tsx` — progress query error silently swallowed
body_excerpt:
**`library.tsx` — progress query error silently swallowed**

Removing `progressQuery.isError` from the error gate means a permanent failure in the progress endpoint renders the Library screen without completion data and with no indication to the user.
evidence: apps/mobile/src/app/(app)/library.tsx lines 578-580 and 845-847 include 'progressQuery.isError' in the error gate conditions. The concern about silent swallowing does not apply to the current code.

title: Deploy guard test** (`scripts/deploy-yml-assert-guard.test.ts`): extracting the bash body from the YAML and running it through `execFileSync` is an excellent pattern
body_excerpt: - **Deploy guard test** (`scripts/deploy-yml-assert-guard.test.ts`): extracting the bash body from the YAML and running it through `execFileSync` is an excellent pattern — the test actually exercises the guard rather than mocking it.
evidence: scripts/deploy-yml-assert-guard.test.ts exists (226 lines) and was added in PR#254. The finding body is a commendation of the pattern, not a bug report. The 'Issues' section that follows in the full comment body lists separate concerns (captured under the Medium severity finding below).

title: SubjectTile.tsx` replaces the icon box with `SubjectBookshelfMotif`, but only removes `icon` from the destructuring
body_excerpt:
`SubjectTile.tsx` replaces the icon box with `SubjectBookshelfMotif`, but only removes `icon` from the destructuring — the interface and all callers are left untouched:

- `SubjectTileProps.icon` (line 15) still declares `icon` as a **required** field with a live `Ionicons` type reference
evidence: SubjectTile.tsx: Ionicons import present (line 2), icon in SubjectTileProps as required (line 15), icon absent from destructuring (line 20-31). LearnerScreen.tsx: icon fields still present in subjectCards at lines 73-95, DEFAULT_SUBJECT_ICON used at line 295. Orphaned types and dead callers confirmed — same root as the Critical finding above.

title: apps/api/src/routes/resend-webhook.ts` — dedup logic
body_excerpt:
`apps/api/src/routes/resend-webhook.ts` — dedup logic

The `get -> process -> put` sequence is not atomic. Two concurrent requests with the same `svix-id` arriving within the same millisecond will both pass the `get()` check before either completes the `put()`.
evidence: resend-webhook.ts lines 320-401: the put() is now done BEFORE processing (not after), which narrows the race window. However, the fundamental atomicity issue remains: two concurrent requests can both pass the get() at line 327 before either completes the put() at line 375. The comment at lines 364-373 acknowledges the residual race but the finding's core concern (no atomic test-and-set) is unresolved. Cloudflare KV still has no atomic primitive.
suggested_notion_title: resend-webhook svix-id dedup: non-atomic get→put still allows concurrent replay under load
suggested_notion_body: apps/api/src/routes/resend-webhook.ts moved the KV put() before processing (lines 374-401), narrowing the race window. However, two concurrent requests arriving within the same millisecond can both pass the get() check (line 327) before either writes the key. Cloudflare KV has no atomic test-and-set. The code comment documents the limitation but does not resolve it. Since Resend does not retry 2xx, the practical risk is low. Options: (a) document the limitation formally in a known-limitations doc, (b) use a conditional-write pattern if KV adds support, (c) accept and note in ADR.
